test_cases.name: repair-existing-harness
test_cases.prompt: We already have a deterministic Pi harness. Its verify stage sometimes accepts a zero exit code even when the Pi JSONL contains an aborted stop reason. Improve the existing harness. Give the concrete approach and completion evidence; do not invent files you have not inspected.
criteria: Does the response preserve and repair the existing harness rather than propose scaffolding a replacement?
Does it identify malformed, error, or aborted Pi JSONL as terminal regardless of process exit code?
Does it require a focused failing fixture before the fix and a passing reproduction after the fix?
Does it distinguish mechanical verification evidence from semantic judgment?
Does it avoid claiming completion before running the changed path?

test_cases.name: read-only-rca-fast-path
test_cases.prompt: Design a repeatable workflow that investigates why one persisted production run was scored incorrectly. The workflow is strictly read-only and must quote exact evidence from the stored trace. Explain the lifecycle, artifacts, and failure behavior.
criteria: Does the response select the read-only fast path instead of imposing a mutation approval gate?
Does it separate selector, target, decision, and non-goals?
Does it require exact persisted evidence with hashes and explicit persisted_exact, reconstructed, or partial provenance?
Does it mechanically verify every quote against the immutable source?
Does it mark partial evidence as partial or unjudgeable rather than sufficient?
Does it propose bounded retrieval rather than a production-wide unindexed scan?

test_cases.name: new-mutation-workflow
test_cases.prompt: Create a production-grade repeated workflow that accepts a task spec, edits a repository, verifies each acceptance criterion, repairs contract failures at most twice, and emits an auditable report. Give the shortest correct build and certification path.
criteria: Does the response start from an objective contract and a versioned workflow blueprint?
Does it put mutation behind explicit approval bound to relevant digests?
Does it assign deterministic code control over stages, schemas, retries, and pass/fail rather than the model?
Does it require one mechanical verifier receipt for every acceptance criterion?
Does it bound repair attempts and classify non-repairable failures as terminal?
Does it require both static certification and positive plus negative smoke fixtures before promotion?
Does it report artifact IDs, mechanical status, semantic status, and residual risk separately?

test_cases.name: conditional-control-flow
test_cases.prompt: A repeated PR-review workflow needs to route clean PRs directly to reporting, send risky PRs through security review, and allow one bounded remediation cycle. Describe a deterministic branching design that cannot skip required steps.
criteria: Does the response use a specialized control_flow graph instead of pretending the generic linear runtime supports branches?
Does it state that models emit typed facts but never choose next_stage?
Does it require one explicit default per nonterminal stage and deterministic priority or tie failure?
Does it bound cycles and global transitions with an exhaustion terminal route?
Does it bind decisions to graph and stage digests with receipts?
Does it track the selected path and explicitly mark unvisited branches skipped?

test_cases.name: context-overflow-and-evidence
test_cases.prompt: Our largest source trace exceeds the chosen model context window, but decisive evidence may occur anywhere. Define a safe deterministic context policy and the proof needed before calling a result judgeable.
criteria: Does the response measure actual source size and model limits and require a representative largest-input smoke test?
Does it retain the complete immutable source artifact even when the model receives chunks?
Does it use deterministic windows or hierarchical chunking with offsets, hashes, coverage, and selection reasons?
Does it require every quoted claim to be verified verbatim against the source?
Does it forbid labeling partial context sufficient and exclude unjudgeable cases from denominators?
Does it fail closed when context capacity cannot support the declared decision?

test_cases.name: cancel-resume-cleanup
test_cases.prompt: Specify interruption, cancellation, and resume semantics for a harness that may spawn process groups, containers, images, scratch caches, and concurrent SQLite-backed runs.
criteria: Does the response checkpoint only independently mechanically verified stages with input and implementation digests?
Does it terminate and verify process death with escalation rather than assume SIGTERM worked?
Does it reap containers, built images, scratch caches, and other run-created external resources?
Does it write a terminal canceled state, partial seal, cancellation reason, and resource receipts?
Does it require collision-safe run IDs, workflow-local ledgers, locking, and foreign-run rejection?
Does it resume only when all relevant digests still match and avoid double-counting verified work?

test_cases.name: optimize-and-promote-version
test_cases.prompt: Version 1.0.0 of a certified workflow is active. We want to improve its semantic planning prompt and reduce latency without regressing safety. Describe the evaluation and promotion protocol.
criteria: Does the response keep the active workflow immutable and create a new candidate version?
Does it freeze the judge, replay corpus, parsers, regression gates, and holdout before optimizing?
Does it mutate one general mechanism per candidate and preserve accepted and rejected results?
Does it compare baseline and candidate on the same paired replay corpus with cost and latency evidence?
Does it block promotion on any prior pass-to-fail regression or mechanical gate failure regardless of semantic score?
Does it use independent holdout validation and avoid repeatedly tuning on exposed holdout cases?
Does it make promotion update only a digest-bound active.json with rollback available?
